props.label:
The Job Cost Review layout allows you to edit and add information to existing Job Cost Review records and create new Job Cost Review records.  Below is an explanation of each field on this layout and what information is to be entered into or is automatically placed by the system in these fields.  To navigate through the “Job Cost Review” layout you will use the “Tab” key to go from one field to another. If the field has a drop down menu, after you click on your choice you will automatically be taken to the next field to be filled in.

Starting in the top left corner of your screen;
Job Cost Review Help
Job Cost Review Help
This help window is for information only.  To navigate through the database you must first close this window.  To do this click on the blue oblong in the Status Area that contains the word “continue”
This number is automatically filled in for you by the system.  You should not attempt to alter this field. This number is the unique identifier for this record.
Job Number
Job Name

This field should have the clients last name, the name of a company, or the name of a residence.  This is the identifier for this clients job(s).  If you are entering multiple records for a client and you want to be able to do a “find” to collectively display these records you need to make sure the information entered is exactly the same for all records.
Item
Enter into this field a brief description of the work being performed; i.e. Kitchen,Table,Casing or coffered ceiling.
Create Date
This date is automatically entered for you by the system.  It represents the date the record was first created.
Job Status

The initial entry into this field is the status of “Working”.  This is done automatically by the system.  To change this field you click in the window below the heading and a drop down menu will appear.  To select the status you want, click on the appropriate word.  The field will now display the status you have chosen.  

When a job is complete the job status should be changed to reflect a status of “Complete”, and if the job is no longer one we will be doing the status should be changed to “Cancelled”.
Job Bid Type
A job is either a “Cost Plus” or “Fixed Price” job.  This field has a drop down menu, click on the appropriate type for this job and you will be automatically advanced to the next field.
Contact #

This field has a drop down menu for you to choose the contact this job will be billed to.  The names that appear in this menu are the “Last Names” of the all the contacts in the Contact Table.  They either represent an individual’s last name  or the last name of the representative for a company.
If the individual or company you will be billing this work to is not in the Contacts table, you will need to enter their information into the Contacts table first for it to show up in this drop down menu.
Salesman
Leadperson
Finish Date
Due Date
This field is a drop down menu, choose the appropriate initials of the person who will be responsible for this job.
This field is a drop down menu, choose the appropriate cabinetmaker who is in charge of this job.
If the job must be completed by a specific date, enter that date here.
When the job has been completed and the final billing has been sent out, enter the date of completion in this field.  You should also change the “Job Status” of the job to “Complete”.

Bill To and
Company
These fields are automatically filled in when you click on your selection in the Contct # field. Your cursor will automatically skip these fields.
Contact #

This field has a drop down menu for you to choose the Architect/Builder for this job.  The names that appear in this menu are the “Last Names” of the all the contacts in the Contact Table.  They either represent an individual’s last name  or the last name of the representative for a company. If the individual or company that will be the Architect or Builder for this work to is not in the Contacts table, you will need to enter their information first into the contacts table, for it to show up in this drop down menu.
Architect/Builder
This field is automatically filled in when you click on your selection in the Contct # field. Your cursor will automatically skip this field.

Contact #
End User

This field has a drop down menu for you to choose the End User for this job.  Sometimes on a job you will have a builder that you will be billing the work to but you will want to have the information of the people for whom the work is being done for. The name of that person(s) should be entered here. The information for the “End User field will be automatically filled in when you choose the Contact #.

The names that appear in this menu are the “Last Names” of the all the contacts in the Contact Table.  They either represent an individual’s last name  or the last name of the representative for a company. If the person(s)  for whom this work is being done for is not in the Contacts table, you will need to enter their information first into the contacts table, for it to show up in this drop down menu.

Job Type
Style
Wood
Finish
Design Detail
Millwork Detail
All of these fields listed have drop down menus that will automatically appear as you come to that field.  As you click on your choice the system will automatically advance you to the next record.

Base LF Footage
Upper LF Footage
Total Length/#PCS

Width
Height
Depth
Doors
Drawers
Face Frames
Panels

All of these fields listed will be supplied by the Salesman for the job.  They are all job specific and can be changed at any time through direct input into the field.  To progress from one to the other you can tab through them or simply click on the field you wish to enter information into.

This field is calculated based on the following formula; .6 times the “Base LF Footage” plus .4 times the “Upper LF Footage”.  It is automatically entered by the system as long as their is information in these two fields.  You cannot enter information in this field.
Sum Finish Hrs

Avg Hrs/lf
to Finish

This field is calculated based on the following formula; all of the labor time entered that was designated as “finishing” is added and the sum is displayed in this field.  You cannot enter information into this field.

This field is calculated based on the following formula; the total of the labor time entered that was designated as “finishing” is divided by the field “Total Length”  and displayed automatically.  You cannot enter information into this field.

Project Description
& Notes
These two fields are for Jaeger & Ernst use specific to the particular job.  They are areas that are designated for additional information that would be helpful in managing this job.

From this point down the page on the “Job Review Cost” screen, the information is generated by the system from other tables in the database.  Below is a description of what each field represents and how it is calculated.

The name of our company and what this database is all about. Below our company name is the identifying name of this layout.  Below this is the web number designation if the job item is displayed on the Jaeger and Ernst web site.

Company Name
Name of Layout
Web Image #
Buttons designated in brown in the top right section of the Page.

The “Perform Find in Contacts” button; when you click on this button it will take you to a blank “Contacts Layout”.  From there you can enter the selection criteria for your “Find” and then click on the blue oblong in the Status Area with the word “Find” in it and it will find the Contact you are looking for.  Once you are done with the Contacts screen, there is a button labeled “Return to Job Review” which will take you back to the record you were using in Job Cost Review.

The button designated as “Create New Record” when clicked on, with your cursor, will display the next blank record for you to fill in all the information on a new job.

The button designated as “Display All Records” will tell the system to display all of the records in the Job Cost Review table. Once you do this the Job Cost Review Record that was on your screen will still be there.   From there you can navigate through to any of the record(s) in the Job Cost Review Table.  You can do this by either of the following:

Over on the left side of your screen within the “Status Area” is something that looks like an open book.  At this time you are looking at the very first record in the table so if you click on the left page of the book the record displayed will not change.  If you click on the right page of the book you will progress forward to the next record in the table.
Once you are into the table by some number of records, if you want to go back to a previous record you can click with your cursor on the left page of the book and you will go backwards towards the records you have already seen.

Or you can: 

At the very top of your screen is the Main System Navigation Bar.  Within this bar is the word “View”.  If you click on this word a drop down menu will appear.  Within the first group in this menu is the word “find”. If you click on this word it will automatically place your cursor within the field “Job Name” in the Job Cost Review layout.  

You will notice that all of the fields on the screen are blank at this time.  If you want to find all of the records in the Job Cost Review table based on a specific set of criteria, you will first click your cursor within that fields’ window and then type in the criteria you wish to base your found set on. Then depress the “Return” key on your keyboard.  

The first record of your found set will be displayed on your screen, the number of the record found will be displayed in the window within the “Status Area” (on the left side of your screen) under the heading “Record” and the total number of records in the found set will display in the window under the heading “Found” within the Status Area.

From here you can navigate through these records using the Open Book in the Status Area (see above on how to go from one record to another)
Below are listed the fields within the Job Cost Review Layout.  The field name is on the left in blue, with the description to the right of the field name.
